meta.name: WI Whiteley Index
meta.description: The WI is a 14-item self-report scale that assesses the severity of health anxiety (hypochondriasis). It measures excessive preoccupation with bodily symptoms and unreasonable fear of disease, and is a classic tool for assessing health anxiety.
meta.author: Pilowsky (1967)
meta.reference: Pilowsky I. Dimensions of hypochondriasis. Br J Psychiatry. 1967;113(494):89-93.
meta.instruction: Please rate each item based on your actual feelings.

questions.text: Do you often worry about the possibility that you have a serious illness
Are you bothered by many different symptoms
Do you often notice various aches and pains in different parts of your body
Are you overly concerned about your health
Do you often have unusual bodily symptoms
When you learn about an illness, do you worry about getting it yourself
If you feel ill, does it bother you when someone says you look well
Do you have pain in many different parts of your body
Do you often worry about something being wrong with your body
Do you find it hard to believe the doctor when told there is nothing wrong
Even when tests are normal, do you still worry about your health
When others tell you nothing is wrong, do you still worry about your symptoms
Do you find yourself often thinking about your body
Do you visit doctors more frequently than others your age

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your health anxiety level is within the normal range. Your concern about physical health is appropriate. This indicates that your anxiety levels are within the normal range, and your psychological state is relatively calm.
suggestions: Continue maintaining reasonable health awareness
Get regular check-ups to maintain your health
Continue maintaining your healthy lifestyle and coping strategies
Practice daily relaxation activities such as deep breathing or stretching
Keep up a regular exercise routine

ranges.label: Mild Health Anxiety
ranges.description: You have some degree of health anxiety and may occasionally worry excessively about bodily symptoms. Mild tension or worry is common in everyday life. Simple self-regulation techniques can often help manage these feelings.
suggestions: Learn to distinguish normal bodily sensations from disease signals
Avoid excessive online searching about diseases
Practice mindfulness and relaxation techniques to reduce body-focused attention
If worry persists, consider consulting a doctor
Learn and practice progressive muscle relaxation

ranges.label: Moderate Health Anxiety
ranges.description: Your health anxiety level is elevated. Excessive attention to bodily symptoms may affect daily life. At this level, you may find it difficult to relax, experience frequent worry, and notice effects on sleep or concentration.
suggestions: Consider seeking psychological counseling
Cognitive-behavioral therapy is very effective for health anxiety
Reduce repeated medical visits and excessive testing
Learn techniques for accepting uncertainty
Consider consulting a counselor or psychiatrist

ranges.label: Severe Health Anxiety
ranges.description: Your health anxiety level is very high. Excessive worry about illness may seriously affect your quality of life. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional mental health help
Cognitive-behavioral therapy is the first-line treatment for health anxiety
Avoid repeated medical visits and tests for reassurance
Discuss with a professional whether medication may be helpful
Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
